speaker: MATT
raw_text: You guys make your way back to the Leaky Tap, heading up to your rooms, and eventually find yourselves falling to sleep. However, the distant thunder rolls continue to quake the muffled interior of the rooms you're at, the lightning strikes flashing through the rooms that have a respective window, and the heavy impacts causing you to wake up in the middle of the evening at times with a restless bit of sleep. A faint drip of wayward rain seems to have collected and found its way through the roof of Beau and Jester's room, and the pattering in the corner of this small, building puddle causes you to frustratingly go and collect it, find something to at least keep it from making that noise throughout the evening.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Hey! It's like we don't even have to bathe, now.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I love the rain.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: You know, if we're just going back-- Pumat Sol, what was the name of his spot?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: It was--

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: A soul asylum?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: No, it wasn't.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: That could've been a cool name, though.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Invulnerable Vagrant.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Oh, yeah. How far away is the Invulnerable Vagrant?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: From where you are now, exiting the Evening Nip, it's maybe a 20-minute, 30-minute walk to the middle of the Pentamarket.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Is it late evening, like pre-midnight, or?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It's pretty late evening, it's pushing on midnight, yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: You know what, never mind, I'll do it another day.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Should we go to sleep?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Let's go to sleep.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Sleep, yes.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Okay. Eventually, you all come to consciousness again, sore from the action of the previous day, but the daylight greets you, muddled as it is from the darkened grey raining skies that greet you. The thunderstorm is still passing through, and a quick glance out through the window into the main thoroughfare, you can see the streets are muddied, and in some places gathered into small pools and flooded puddles, but Yasha is gone.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: They really are rat balloons.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Little rat balloons, and one of them exploding with poison coming out of it.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You see a hint of a smile at the edge of her lips, and she gives a nod. "That's pretty good. I could never draw."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: No? Do you want me to draw you right now? I'll do it, okay!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You see her pull back a bit. Another flash, a nearby bolt causes the sky to light up. Yasha's unfazed, and her attention shifts back to the glass of the window again, a brief moment of trance-like attention before, "You should try to sleep."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: You all right?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: She gives a nod and sits back down, closes her eyes, still sitting in a meditative position on the ground.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I draw her lit up by the lightning and then I go to sleep.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "I need to go." He pushes past you, and as he begins to exit the chamber, he goes, "I'm all right, I'm good, thank you this time, good bye," and darts out into the rain.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh no. Perception? 18.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It is Shallow Breaths.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Breasts, or breaths?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Breaths.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Heaving.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Heaving breasts.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: (breathes deeply)

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Ooh, Shallow Breaths, that's a good one.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: As the guy turns around, you can see him. He's mid to late thirties, you would expect, dark hair slicked back, the hairline pulling back a bit, male pattern baldness-style, human, and as soon he whips around: "Oh, I'm just perusing."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: It's not one of your favorites?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I don't know, I've not read it, I couldn't tell you."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: What's your name?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: At this point, you see Ulog make his way up and take a different table at the far corner. Then slowly, over the next 15 or so minutes, the other three exit the building.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: You were down there a really long time.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: What is the benefit for us?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Well, my takeaway is that they want to unseat some of the corrupted seats of power here in the city. If that's true, if there is an abuse of power, I'm all for removing those corrupted seats and putting people of worth in their place. I'm also not keen on taking the word of someone else without getting some evidence of our own.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I agree. Who knows? We can't be replacing someone corrupt with someone even worse.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: We should follow one of them.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, we don't know anything about this Dolan, this Horris' motives, or their agenda.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: We don't. While we're following them, perhaps we should try and find out what we can about this-- Lean in closer. Closer. This High-Richter Prucine.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Have the ones downstairs already left?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I don't know.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: You guys all eventually find yourselves to rest for the evening, awaking in the early morning. The chilled morning air comes through. The sky: still mostly gray, with patches of blue that poke through. No rain, it's still a little chilled. There's a bit of fog burning off by the time you awaken and look out your room's window. The morning is yours, what do you wish to do?
trigger_phrase: find yourselves to rest for the evening

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Man, that was more than I thought.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: That was very interesting.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: What do you make of all that?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Oh, I don't know. People are strange. Anyone who looks like us, you collect a few interesting enemies on the way, even at her age.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Are you praying over your swords tonight?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Of course. Every night.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: All right. Can I join you?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Sure.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Great.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Come on, I'm just putting up the thread, come to bed. Come to bed.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Do you trust Fjord and Molly?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: No. No, I don't.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Me neither.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: (poof) Alarm.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: And then Oskar falls in love with her! And he carries her across a field, and they love each other so much!

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: That's so great.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: It's the most beautiful story, Beau!

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Can we go to sleep, now?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, the hard turn!

speaker: MATT

The sky: still mostly gray, with patches of blue that poke through. No rain, it's still a little chilled. There's a bit of fog burning off by the time you awaken and look out your room's window. The morning is yours, what do you wish to do?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Sure.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Great.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: No. No, I don't.

preceding_turns.text: Me neither.
